text: Incorporate OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol as an anchor model in the harness adversarial review process. Community consensus is that GPT-5.6 Sol performs high-quality reviews, so it should be added as an anchor/reviewer model alongside existing models in the multi-model adversarial verification flow (verify-harness skill and any plan-review/review persona model routing that supports cross-model diversity). Ensure it is wired as a first-class anchor reviewer, remains environment-agnostic, and preserves consensus-based finding assembly. Scope should cover: where anchor/reviewer models are configured, how GPT-5.6 Sol is selected/routed, and any docs/templates that enumerate review models.

Rename agent definition files: .ship.agent.md -> _ship.agent.md and .stage.agent.md -> _stage.agent.md. Apply to both the installed mirrors under .github/agents/ and the source templates under templates/agents/ (.ship.agent.md.tmpl -> _ship.agent.md.tmpl, .stage.agent.md.tmpl -> _stage.agent.md.tmpl). Likewise update the frontmatter name: property for each so the leading '.' becomes a leading '_' (e.g., name: .Ship -> _Ship, name: .Stage -> _Stage). Scope must also sweep all cross-references to the old filenames/paths and the old name values across agents, skills, instructions, prompts, docs, schemas, the harness manifest checksums, and any installer/tuner logic that emits or references these filenames, so nothing points at the old dot-prefixed names after the rename.

Consider enforcing the /compact command after each PR merge as a workflow protocol. Evaluate whether post-merge context compaction should be a mandatory workflow step (e.g., in Ship post-merge closure / P-017 dark-mode closure and the two-agent Stage/Ship handoff) so that context is consolidated after every merge. Touch points likely include workflow-policies (new P-0xx candidate), the compact-context skill wiring, and Ship/Orchestrator closure sequencing.

Evaluate the backlogit workspace's evolved multi-persona plan-review skill (C:\Source\GitHub\backlogit\.github\skills\plan-review\SKILL.md) against autoharness's current plan-review template (templates/skills/plan-review/SKILL.md.tmpl) and consider incorporating/merging the enhancements. FINDING: autoharness DOES already have a plan-review skill template with the same multi-persona structure (Constitution Reviewer, stack Reviewer, Scope Boundary Auditor, Learnings Researcher, plus cross-model personas). The backlogit copy is NOT merely a rendered instance of that template — it has substantively diverged (~156 -> ~287 lines) and adds three sections the autoharness template currently lacks: (1) "Dispatch Capability and Declared Degradation" — makes the review gate capability-aware so it is satisfiable in every environment (Copilot CLI `task` tool / VS Code agents vs environments without sub-agent dispatch) and never silently skips its dispatch step; (2) "Relationship to P-012" — applies P-012's declared-degradation principle (probe before relying, declare degraded mode, never fall back silently) to reviewer sub-agent dispatch, which is not a backlog-registry tool, and flags formalizing this as an explicit P-012 capability clause; (3) "Persona rubric adapter (authoritative plan-review lens)" — a mechanism where each persona = identity .agent.md file + the plan-focused Focus column, normalized to mergeable P0-P3 findings, so both dispatch (sub-agent) and inline modes resolve IDENTICAL rubrics instead of drifting into code-review vs plan-review behavior; includes a persona display-name -> identity-file -> plan-lens -> trigger mapping table. RECOMMENDATION: back-port these three enhancements into templates/skills/plan-review/SKILL.md.tmpl, keeping them environment-agnostic and variable-parameterized ({{DOCS_PLANS}}, stack/persona placeholders, no backlogit-specific literals). Check companion impact on: plan-harden and harvest skill templates, the workflow-policies P-012 template (potential new capability clause), and any review-persona agent templates referenced by the adapter table. Also confirm whether the backlogit copy carries LF/CRLF or naming assumptions that must be normalized for the environment-agnostic template. Cross-check against the related stash entry E929B1C9 (adding GPT-5.6 Sol as an anchor reviewer model) since both touch multi-model review routing.

Finish + ship the agent-intercom opt-in / non-MCP capability-pack reclassification (DEFERRED to a later shipment). Reclassify agent-intercom from a full-preset default to an opt-in extra add-on, and reflect that it no longer supports an MCP server (operates over a non-MCP intercom/ACP tool surface); it remains a woven overlay when explicitly enabled, not a detached single-file add-on. STATUS: only the 13-file patch portion is complete (targeted suites green locally); the change is NOT fully done. The deploy wrappers still make agent-intercom non-opt-in and MUST be fixed before shipment. DURABLE ARTIFACTS (committed, no local state needed): apply docs/deferred/agent-intercom-opt-in-nonmcp.patch on a fresh feature branch, and follow the self-contained plan in docs/deferred/agent-intercom-opt-in-nonmcp.md (full 13-file change list, the "Additional required scope - deploy wrappers" section, resume steps, checksum-refresh + verify_workspace instructions). REMAINING WORK (not in the patch): scripts/deploy-harness.sh and scripts/deploy-harness.ps1 default omitted pack input to all and enumerate every registry pack for non-starter presets without consulting default_in_preset, so default_in_preset:[] alone does NOT make agent-intercom opt-in; the wrappers must distinguish an omitted pack input from an explicit all, resolve omitted input to the selected preset default_in_preset members, keep explicit all meaning all packs, parse default_in_preset, and add wrapper tests. RESUME: git checkout -b feat/agent-intercom-opt-in-nonmcp origin/main; git apply --3way docs/deferred/agent-intercom-opt-in-nonmcp.patch; implement the deploy-wrapper scope + wrapper tests (see "Additional required scope"); re-run pytest tests/test_capability_pack_registry.py tests/test_graphtor_docs_full_suite.py tests/test_manifest_capability_pack_enum.py plus the new wrapper tests; refresh the 3 manifest checksums (raw-bytes sha256 of CRLF files); run verify_workspace; then PR as its own shipment. Do NOT bundle with the 079 telemetry ship work.

Follow-up for 088-F compression shipment / 088.004-T: add a mandatory failure-content detector plus negative controls. The current plan and task only assert that postToolUseFailure output is left untouched, but a shell/CI command that fails inside a SUCCESSFUL tool result arrives via postToolUse.textResultForLlm and is rewritable; the compressor must decline (not compress) successful tool results that embed a non-zero exit code, stderr, a stack trace, or a gate verdict. Add the detector and negative-control tests to both the compression experiment plan task breakdown and the queued 088.004-T acceptance criteria. Source: PR #226 Copilot review (compression-plan:84). Deferred from PR #226 to keep spec-expansion in the 088-F shipment rather than a backlog/docs reconciliation PR.

Genericize Ship-agent 092-S sizing-readiness derivation: the ship agent template (templates/agents/.ship.agent.md.tmpl) and its installed mirror (.github/agents/.ship.agent.md) hard-code 079.013-T/079.015-T/092-S literals for execution-ready sizing guardrails. PR 227 round-2 Copilot t4/t5 flagged that readiness should derive from declared task deps/status rather than embedded IDs. Deferred because the ratified test test_092s_execution_ready_guardrails_are_documented_in_ship_agents (tests/test_telemetry_ship_lifecycle.py:153-162) currently MANDATES those literals in both template and mirror, so genericizing is a spec change requiring the test contract to be updated first. Scope: replace literal IDs with a derivation rule + update the ratified test.

Telemetry: Ship should supply an explicit close 	imestamp in the 	elemetry record close payload so context-based epochs bucket by close time. Currently _merge_telemetry_context_payload falls back to context captured_at (begin time) when the close payload omits a timestamp, because a context-replayable close MUST hash to a stable digest across retries (ratified idempotency contract: test_record_context_idempotency_and_conflict_outcomes). An explicit close timestamp from the harness would satisfy both close-time accuracy (Copilot PR227 r3-1) and replay determinism. Fold into the ship-agent sizing/telemetry-close genericization work (see 638AA991).
